meta.title: Daggerheart Heritage (Ancestries & Communities)
meta.license: DPCGL. SRD content (c) Critical Role, LLC.
notes: Feature text is verbatim. Flavor 'description' fields are ABRIDGED for brevity (mechanics are unaffected); full flavor is at each url.
Heritage = ancestry + community (Demiplane bundles these in one builder step).

ancestries.name: Clank
ancestries.description: Clanks are sentient mechanical beings built from a variety of materials, including metal, wood, and stone. They can resemble humanoids, animals, or even inanimate objects.

features.name: Purposeful Design
features.text: Decide who made you and for what purpose. At character creation, choose one of your Experiences that best aligns with this purpose and gain a permanent +1 bonus to it.

features.name: Efficient
features.text: When you take a short rest, you can choose a long rest move instead of a short rest move.

ancestries.name: Drakona
ancestries.description: Drakona resemble wingless dragons in humanoid form and possess a powerful elemental breath. All drakona have thick scales that provide excellent natural armor.

features.name: Scales
features.text: Your scales act as natural protection. When you would take Severe damage, you can mark a Stress to mark 1 fewer Hit Points.

features.name: Elemental Breath
features.text: Choose an element for your breath (such as electricity, fire, or ice). You can use this breath against a target or group of targets within Very Close range, treating it as an Instinct weapon that deals d8 magic damage using your Proficiency.

ancestries.name: Dwarf
ancestries.description: Dwarves are most easily recognized as short humanoids with square frames, dense musculature, and thick hair. Their skin and nails contain a high amount of keratin, making them naturally resilient.

features.name: Thick Skin
features.text: When you take Minor damage, you can mark 2 Stress instead of marking a Hit Point.

features.name: Increased Fortitude
features.text: Spend 3 Hope to halve incoming physical damage.

ancestries.name: Elf
ancestries.description: Elves are typically tall humanoids with pointed ears and acutely attuned senses. All elves have the ability to drop into a celestial trance, rather than sleep.

features.name: Quick Reactions
features.text: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on a reaction roll.

features.name: Celestial Trance
features.text: During a rest, you can drop into a trance to choose an additional downtime move.

ancestries.name: Faerie
ancestries.description: Faeries are winged humanoid creatures with insectile features. All faeries possess membranous wings and they each go through a process of metamorphosis.

features.name: Luckbender
features.text: Once per session, after you or a willing ally within Close range makes an action roll, you can spend 3 Hope to reroll the Duality Dice.

features.name: Wings
features.text: You can fly. While flying, you can mark a Stress after an adversary makes an attack against you to gain a +2 bonus to your Evasion against that attack.

ancestries.name: Faun
ancestries.description: Fauns resemble humanoid goats with curving horns, square pupils, and cloven hooves. They are known for their ability to deliver powerful blows with their split hooves.

features.name: Caprine Leap
features.text: You can leap anywhere within Close range as though you were using normal movement, allowing you to vault obstacles, jump across gaps, or scale barriers with ease.

features.name: Kick
features.text: When you succeed on an attack against a target within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to kick yourself off them, dealing an extra 2d6 damage and knocking back either yourself or the target to Very Close range.

ancestries.name: Firbolg
ancestries.description: Firbolgs are bovine humanoids typically recognized by their broad noses and long, drooping ears. They are tall and muscular creatures and possess remarkable strength.

features.name: Charge
features.text: When you succeed on an Agility Roll to move from Far or Very Far range into Melee range with one or more targets, you can mark a Stress to deal 1d12 physical damage to all targets within Melee range.

features.name: Unshakable
features.text: When you would mark a Stress, roll a d6. On a result of 6, don't mark it.

ancestries.name: Fungril
ancestries.description: Fungril resemble humanoid mushrooms. They can communicate nonverbally, and many use a mycelial array to chemically exchange information with other fungril across long distances.

features.name: Fungril Network
features.text: Make an Instinct Roll (12) to use your mycelial array to speak with others of your ancestry. On a success, you can communicate across any distance.

features.name: Death Connection
features.text: While touching a corpse that died recently, you can mark a Stress to extract one memory from the corpse related to a specific emotion or sensation of your choice.

ancestries.name: Galapa
ancestries.description: Galapa resemble anthropomorphic turtles with large, domed shells into which they can retract. They can use their shell as a natural shield when defensive measures are needed.

features.name: Shell
features.text: Gain a bonus to your damage thresholds equal to your Proficiency.

features.name: Retract
features.text: Mark a Stress to retract into your shell. While in your shell, you have resistance to physical damage, you have disadvantage on action rolls, and you can't move.

ancestries.name: Giant
ancestries.description: Giants are towering humanoids with broad shoulders, long arms, and one to three eyes. Those with a single eye are commonly known as cyclops.

features.name: Endurance
features.text: Gain an additional Hit Point slot at character creation.

features.name: Reach
features.text: Treat any weapon, ability, spell, or other feature that has a Melee range as though it has a Very Close range instead.

ancestries.name: Goblin
ancestries.description: Goblins are small humanoids easily recognizable by their large eyes and massive membranous ears. With keen hearing and sharp eyesight, they perceive details at great distances and in darkness.

features.name: Surefooted
features.text: You ignore disadvantage on Agility Rolls.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.text: Once per rest, mark a Stress to force an adversary to reroll an attack against you or an ally within Very Close range.

ancestries.name: Halfling
ancestries.description: Halflings are small humanoids with large hairy feet and prominent rounded ears. They are naturally attuned to the magnetic fields of the Mortal Realm, granting them a strong internal compass.

features.name: Luckbringer
features.text: At the start of each session, everyone in your party gains a Hope.

features.name: Internal Compass
features.text: When you roll a 1 on your Hope Die, you can reroll it.

ancestries.name: Human
ancestries.description: Humans are most easily recognized by their dexterous hands, rounded ears, and bodies built for endurance. They are physically adaptable and adjust to harsh climates with relative ease.

features.name: High Stamina
features.text: Gain an additional Stress slot at character creation.

features.name: Adaptability
features.text: When you fail a roll that utilized one of your Experiences, you can mark a Stress to reroll.

ancestries.name: Infernis
ancestries.description: Infernis are humanoids who possess sharp canine teeth, pointed ears, and horns. They are the descendants of demons from the Circles Below.

features.name: Fearless
features.text: When you roll with Fear, you can mark 2 Stress to change it into a roll with Hope instead.

features.name: Dread Visage
features.text: You have advantage on rolls to intimidate hostile creatures.

ancestries.name: Katari
ancestries.description: Katari are feline humanoids with retractable claws, vertically slit pupils, and high, triangular ears. Their ears can swivel nearly 180 degrees to detect sound.

features.name: Feline Instincts
features.text: When you make an Agility Roll, you can spend 2 Hope to reroll your Hope Die.

features.name: Retracting Claws
features.text: Make an Agility Roll to scratch a target within Melee range. On a success, they become temporarily Vulnerable.

ancestries.name: Orc
ancestries.description: Orcs are humanoids most easily recognized by their square features and boar-like tusks that protrude from their lower jaw. Their ears are pointed, and their hair and skin typically have green, blue, pink, or gray tones.

features.name: Sturdy
features.text: When you have 1 Hit Point remaining, attacks against you have disadvantage.

features.name: Tusks
features.text: When you succeed on an attack against a target within Melee range, you can spend a Hope to gore the target with your tusks, dealing an extra 1d6 damage.

ancestries.name: Ribbet
ancestries.description: Ribbets resemble anthropomorphic frogs with protruding eyes and webbed hands and feet. No matter their size, ribbets primarily move by hopping.

features.name: Amphibious
features.text: You can breathe and move naturally underwater.

features.name: Long Tongue
features.text: You can use your long tongue to grab onto things within Close range. Mark a Stress to use your tongue as a Finesse Close weapon that deals d12 physical damage using your Proficiency.

ancestries.name: Simiah
ancestries.description: Simiah resemble anthropomorphic monkeys and apes with long limbs and prehensile feet. They are skilled climbers and can easily transition from bipedal movement to knuckle-walking and climbing.

features.name: Natural Climber
features.text: You have advantage on Agility Rolls that involve balancing and climbing.

features.name: Nimble
features.text: Gain a permanent +1 bonus to your Evasion at character creation.

communities.name: Highborne
communities.description: You're accustomed to a life of elegance, opulence, and prestige within the upper echelons of society. Highborne place great value on titles and possessions, and often control political and economic status through their wealth.

feature.name: Privilege
feature.text: You have advantage on rolls to consort with nobles, negotiate prices, or leverage your reputation to get what you want.

communities.name: Loreborne
communities.description: You're from a society that favors strong academic or political prowess. Loreborne communities highly value knowledge — historical preservation, political advancement, scientific study, skill development, or lore and mythology.

feature.name: Well-Read
feature.text: You have advantage on rolls that involve the history, culture, or politics of a prominent person or place.

communities.name: Orderborne
communities.description: You're from a collective that focuses on discipline or faith, and you uphold a set of principles that reflect your experience there. Orderborne align their members around a common value or goal, such as a god, doctrine, ethos, or trade.

feature.name: Dedicated
feature.text: Record three sayings or values your upbringing instilled in you. Once per rest, when you describe how you're embodying one of these principles through your current action, you can roll a d20 as your Hope Die.

communities.name: Ridgeborne
communities.description: You've called the rocky peaks and sharp cliffs of the mountainside home. Ridgeborne grow up scrambling and climbing, making them sturdy and strong-willed, and are adept at utilizing their high-ground advantage.

feature.name: Steady
feature.text: You have advantage on rolls to traverse dangerous cliffs and ledges, navigate harsh environments, and use your survival knowledge.

communities.name: Seaborne
communities.description: You lived on or near a large body of water. Seaborne communities are closely tied to the ocean tides and the creatures who inhabit them; they learn to fish and swim from a young age.

feature.name: Know the Tide
feature.text: You can sense the ebb and flow of life. When you roll with Fear, place a token on your community card. You can hold a number of tokens equal to your level. Before you make an action roll, you can spend any number of these tokens to gain a +1 bonus to the roll for each token spent. At the end of each session, clear all unspent tokens.

communities.name: Slyborne
communities.description: You come from a group that operates outside the law, including all manner of criminals, grifters, and con artists. Slyborne possess some of the strictest codes of honor, which when broken can result in a terrifying end.

feature.name: Scoundrel
feature.text: You have advantage on rolls to negotiate with criminals, detect lies, or find a safe place to hide.

communities.name: Underborne
communities.description: You're from a subterranean society. Underborne are recognized for boldness and skill enabling great feats of architecture and engineering, and many develop unique nonverbal languages.

feature.name: Low-Light Living
feature.text: When you're in an area with low light or heavy shadow, you have advantage on rolls to hide, investigate, or perceive details within that area.

communities.name: Wanderborne
communities.description: You've lived as a nomad, forgoing a permanent home and experiencing a wide variety of cultures. Wanderborne value information, skills, and connections over material possessions, and are known for unwavering loyalty.

feature.name: Nomadic Pack
feature.text: Add a Nomadic Pack to your inventory. Once per session, you can spend a Hope to reach into this pack and pull out a mundane item that's useful to your situation. Work with the GM to figure out what item you take out.

communities.name: Wildborne
communities.description: You lived deep within the forest. Wildborne are dedicated to conservation of their homelands and integrate their villages with the natural environment, often remaining reclusive and hidden.

feature.name: Lightfoot
feature.text: Your movement is naturally silent. You have advantage on rolls to move without being heard.
